cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking which statement is true about folk medicine based on the provided passage. First, I need to go through each option and check against the text. The passage starts by saying, "Folk medicine keeps the body healthy by using medicines taken from surrounding plants and herbs." So option A says, "It keeps one healthy by using medicines taken from plants and herbs." That seems to directly match the first sentence of the passage. So A is a strong candidate. Option B: "It can treat most kinds of diseases for both man and animals." The passage mentions that folk medicine was used by earliest man and animals, and that animals might use plants for healing. However, does it say it can treat "most kinds" of diseases? The passage doesn't specify the range of diseases it can treat, just that it's used. So B might be an overstatement. Option C: "It is better than other medicines in curing serious diseases." The passage doesn't compare folk medicine to other medicines in terms of effectiveness. It just describes its use and origin. So C is not supported by the text. Option D: "It was a way of body treatment used only in man's early history." The passage says folk medicine goes back to earliest
